data.body:
A flash of white light blinds my sight. When I awake, I am in a hot, barely lit, cramped, space, people shout around me.

I check my pocket, I am Vice Admiral Vasily Arkhipov of the Soviet Navy, in B-59 off the coast of Cuba.

I shake my head, I know what must be done.

Fire the nuclear torpedo.
